page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.6xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. It features 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is rated at an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.hfr7.6xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1579.68, while multi-core performance reaches 24990.45. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1398.59 GB/sec, which drops to 86.33 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 50.31 GB/sec. The system achieves a PassMark CPU score of 25234.51 and a database operations score of 9254.37. Redis SET operations perform at 23.38 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 18.05 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. However, the lack of local storage requires external block storage, which may introduce latency depending on the network configuration. The 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth is a potential constraint for highly distributed systems. This server is configured for memory-optimized workloads, database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and static web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.6xlarge is an x86_64 server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor operating at 3.3 GHz. It features 24 dedicated physical cores, 24 vCPUs, and 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates average-tier capabilities, achieving a PassMark CPU score of 25234.51 and a database operations score of 9254.37. It processes Redis SET operations at 23.38 million operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 18.05 GB/sec. This configuration is engineered for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and caching workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86 server featuring a one-to-eight vCPU-to-RAM ratio and Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.
